about_quotes.speaker: Danny Barker
about_quotes.evidence: Well, we had some fun sitting in Cab's band together, Danny, in that rhythm section. Let's see, it was Bennie Payne and you and Cozy

about_quotes.speaker: Walter P. Thomas
about_quotes.evidence: There was Benny Payne on piano, Morris White on guitar, Leroy Maxey on drums, and I was on bass.

about_quotes.speaker: Eddie Barefield
about_quotes.evidence: And Benny Payne was on piano... And then that's how the band was when I left the band in '35.

about_quotes.speaker: Mona Hinton
about_quotes.evidence: But this was one time he was right. He really hadn’t thrown the spitball, and I’m not going to go into detail to explain who did throw it, because he never admitted that he threw it, but everyone knew who it was. Yeah, and Cab and Dizzy had this little scuffle and Dizzy stuck his knife in Cab’s leg,
about_quotes.doc_title: Mona Hinton interviewed by Milt Fillius and Michael Woods, Scottsdale, Arizona, March 4, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: Walter P. Thomas
about_quotes.evidence: Yeah, he took Prince's place. And we liked Bennie... I don't know. He took Prince's place. He said he joined the band in 1931... and we was playing the Audubon Theatre.

about_quotes.speaker: Jonah Jones
about_quotes.evidence: So now, when the Cab Drivers went out there, that was Tyree, Danny Parker, Benny Payne, Cozy and Milt Hinton.
